sqlite_removal_evidence: No .sql.ts schema files exist — only .pg.sql.ts files
Zero imports from drizzle-orm/sqlite in packages/opencode/src/
AGENTS.md line 3: 'SQLite schemas have been deleted'
package.json #db alias resolves all targets (bun/node/default) to ./src/storage/db.pg.ts

pg_migrations.description: Initial full schema dump. Creates 15 tables with all columns, indexes (10), and foreign keys (10).
Adds runtime_events table (24 columns) + 8 indexes covering session, event_type, ts, actor, run_id, session+ts, campaign_id, lane_id.
Adds 3 coordination tables for cross-session coordination.
Alters coordination_fan_out.task_ids column type to jsonb.

migration_runner.mechanism: For PGlite: reads migration files via readMigrationFiles(), iterates, calls client.exec(sql). For node-postgres: uses drizzle-orm/node-postgres/migrator migrate(). Migration folder resolved as new URL('../../migration-pg', import.meta.url).pathname.

callers.context: makePgAdapter() — fire-and-forget at adapter construction. Errors logged, not propagated.
runMigrations() — CLI db:migrate command. Awaits completion.

migration_runner.called_at_startup_detail: makePgAdapter() in adapter.ts calls applyMigrations(client).catch(...) — runs every time adapter is constructed (app startup). No idempotency guard beyond Drizzle's internal mechanism.
migration_runner.pre_existing_finding_staleness: Finding ses_187bc95c claiming 'applyMigrations defined at db.pg.ts:25 but never called' is STALE — it IS called at adapter.ts:194 and migrate.ts:20

dialect_detection.mechanism: Single dialect: PostgreSQL always. SQLite removed entirely.

backend_selection.logic: If connectionString is ':memory:', starts with '/', or starts with 'file:' → PGlite (embedded PG in WASM). Otherwise → node-postgres (real PG server via pg Pool).
backend_selection.env_var: OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL (in migrate.ts line 9, falls back to ':memory:')
backend_selection.ssl_env_var: OPENCODE_DATABASE_SSL (in migrate.ts line 18, defaults to true unless explicitly 'false')

dialect_detection.db_init_chain: db.ts Database.init() → import {init} from '#db' → db.pg.ts init() → PGlite or node-postgres
dialect_detection.alias_resolution: package.json imports.#db → ./src/storage/db.pg.ts (all targets: bun, node, default)

gap_analysis.verdict: NO GAP — SQLite is intentionally fully removed, PG has 4 migrations covering all tables
evidence: All 19 tables across 4 PG migrations match the 10 .pg.sql.ts schema files
Schema barrel (schema.ts) only re-exports from .pg.sql.ts files
drizzle.config.pg.ts scans ./src/**/*.pg.sql.ts — the canonical schema source
No SQLite schema files, configs, or migration directories remain
Migration runner is active and called at startup + CLI
Test file test/storage/migration.pg.test.ts verifies applyMigrations works end-to-end with PGlite

dead_code_and_debt.issue: Reads from path.join(dir, 'migration') — a directory that no longer exists. This code path would throw at build time if reached.
dead_code_and_debt.recommendation: Remove the dead migration-loading code or point it at migration-pg/ instead.

dead_code_and_debt.issue: Likely has the same dead reference to the deleted migration/ directory.
Variable names still use 'sqlite' terminology (sqliteAdapter, sqliteDb, sqliteTx) but the actual implementation wraps PGlite + remote PG.
dead_code_and_debt.recommendation: Rename to localAdapter/localDb/localTx to reflect PG reality.

dead_code_and_debt.issue: Type exported as SQLiteTransactionOptions with _tag: 'sqlite' — but it's actually used with PGlite (Postgres dialect).
getChannelPath returns 'opencode.db' filename — legacy SQLite convention. Content is PGlite.
dead_code_and_debt.recommendation: Consider renaming to opencode.pg or similar. Low priority since .db is just a file extension convention.

dead_code_and_debt.issue: DatabaseFlags type includes skipMigrations but the flag is never wired through to applyMigrations. The flag is read but ignored.
dead_code_and_debt.recommendation: Either wire skipMigrations to gate applyMigrations in adapter.ts or remove the unused flag.

recommendation: No migration gap exists — the original finding of '20 SQLite vs 1 PG' is outdated. SQLite has been fully removed. There are 4 PG migrations covering all 19 tables across all 10 schema files. However, 6 pieces of dead code referencing SQLite conventions remain and should be cleaned up: build scripts reading from deleted migration/, misleading 'sqlite' variable names in adapter-dual-write.ts, unused skipMigrations flag, and legacy .db file extension naming.
